title: malus × eleyi
intro: Malus × eleyi, also known as the Eley's crabapple, is a rare hybrid species of apple tree that is a cross between Malus baccata and Malus prunifolia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Malus × eleyi include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. The plant is often prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, tonics, or added to salves and ointments for topical application.
